verdict: A flat grid/head-direction path-integrator DETECTS curvature from self-motion alone: the parallel-transport holonomy around a closed loop equals the enclosed area × curvature (Gauss-Bonnet; slope≈1, residual<1%), is exactly 0 in flat space, and makes a flat compass mis-home on a curved world by a computable amount. The signature is never put in the code — it falls out of running a flat integrator on a curved manifold, the non-Euclidean analogue of grid shearing. (The 3-D VOLUME/bat-regime story is covered by grid_3d.py.)
